approach: Fail-closed authentication and independent validation of the R(5,5) order-42 control corpus and provisional two-conflict K43 seed

strategy.mechanism: Freeze exact source-byte discriminators, require them before computation, compare independent Python/direct-combination and C/recursive-bitset evaluators, and cross-check graph6 plumbing with nauty and NetworkX

hypothesis: The authoritative McKay corpus is locally available with the frozen 47,888-byte SHA-256 discriminator and can then reproduce 656 valid, canonically distinct controls under two independent exact checkers.
discriminating_test: Require the local corpus to have SHA-256 067902e853d87b49bcef0d1d4c0e3bbadd238ee18bc65341b079a3ca4780eccb, size 47,888, and 328 records before running any graph check; otherwise record source_preflight_failed with downstream_checks_run=false.
summary: The exact corpus bytes could not be acquired in the managed environment. The source preflight therefore failed closed and no corpus graph was checked. Separately, the provisional K43 matrix control passed both exact checkers and all mutation/rejection tests, and graph6 plumbing passed an exhaustive 1,044-class order-7 adversarial suite against nauty and NetworkX. No Ramsey bound, witness, classification, or exclusion is claimed.
rationale: The epoch produced independently replayable control infrastructure and eliminated parser/complement behavior as an immediate ambiguity, while respecting the source-authentication stop condition. Missing authoritative bytes prevent promotion beyond internal progress.
claims: For the exact unauthenticated transcription with SHA-256 3beebde4f3120337029bb24de1f737f72eab567cbe92c77f79e7206ce4c304e9, two independent checkers agree that graph2 has exactly the K5s {0,15,22,28,39} and {0,15,24,28,39}; deleting 0, 15, 28, or 39 yields a valid order-42 graph.

Across one representative of every 1,044 unlabeled simple graph class on seven vertices, the two graph6 checkers agree exactly, NetworkX agrees on graph signatures, and the complement encoding agrees byte-for-byte with nauty-complg.
The authoritative order-42 corpus was absent locally, so zero corpus or complement instances were checked this epoch.
evidence: CHECKPOINT.md contains exact replay command, hashes, scope limits, and stop conditions
next_steps: Acquire unchanged McKay bytes at sources/r55_42some.g6 while recording timestamp, headers, final URL, byte count, and hash.
If and only if the frozen byte discriminator matches, run the exact authenticated-corpus experiment command recorded in CHECKPOINT.md.
After that gate passes, acquire and authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4, then canonicalize the four valid deletion controls against the supplied 656.
Do not begin unknown-42 or K43 repair search until both raw-source gates pass.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260720-193914-fbe251: provisional K43 dual control passed in 19.857 seconds
.proof-experiments/20260720-194142-be8455: all 1,044 order-7 graph6 classes passed independent plumbing checks in 1.194 seconds
.proof-experiments/20260720-193953-b4e5bd: corpus preflight returned code 2 because the authoritative file was absent; downstream_checks_run=false
independent_checker: Checker A is Python using a separately written matrix/graph6 parser and literal combination enumeration. Checker B is C using separate parsers and recursive bitset clique enumeration in the graph and complement. Nauty 2.8.8+ds-5 independently checked complement bytes, and NetworkX 2.8.8 independently checked parsed signatures.
transfer_insights: Isolated adversarial reconstruction -> independent graph representations should expose shared parser mistakes -> exact agreement was observed across all 1,044 order-7 isomorphism classes.
Fail-closed source authentication -> missing or altered bytes must prevent downstream mathematical claims -> the absent corpus produced an explicit preflight failure with no graph checks.

established_facts.claim: The maintained published range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
established_facts.evidence: 2026 Dynamic Survey and Angeltveit-McKay journal publication
established_facts.scope: Published global R(5,5) bounds as checked on 2026-07-20

established_facts.claim: The custom short-graph6 parsing and complement plumbing agrees with nauty and NetworkX on every unlabeled order-7 graph class.
established_facts.scope: The 1,044 unlabeled simple graph isomorphism classes at order 7

established_facts.claim: The two exact checkers agree on the violation identities and four valid deletions in the provisional K43 transcription.
established_facts.scope: Only input SHA-256 3beebde4f3120337029bb24de1f737f72eab567cbe92c77f79e7206ce4c304e9; publisher equivalence unverified

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat the visible third-party K43 transcription as the authenticated Springer supplementary seed.
ruled_out.reason: Checker agreement proves properties of supplied bytes but not their publisher provenance.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Recover unchanged Springer Supplementary Data 4 and establish an exact transformation or byte correspondence.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Start novel-42 or K43 repair search from the current control packet.
ruled_out.scope: This epoch and any continuation lacking the raw corpus
ruled_out.reason: The required source preflight failed before any corpus validation or canonical comparison.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The authoritative corpus passes the complete dual-check, complement, histogram, degree, and canonical-label gate.

open_leads.description: Authenticate the 328 McKay source records and their complements.
open_leads.rationale: Every novelty and canonical-comparison claim depends on this immutable control set.
open_leads.next_experiment: Place unchanged bytes at sources/r55_42some.g6 and run the authenticated-corpus command in CHECKPOINT.md.

open_leads.rationale: It is the strongest published K43 repair seed, but the current transcription cannot establish publisher provenance.
open_leads.next_experiment: Retrieve the unchanged supplementary TXT, hash it, determine its color convention, and compare exact violation identities under both checkers.

open_leads.description: Enter the unknown-order-42 basin only after controls pass.
open_leads.rationale: Every deletion of a hypothetical K43 witness must lie outside the historically supplied 656.
open_leads.next_experiment: Canonicalize authenticated seed deletions against the 656, then execute a bounded nonlocal destroy-and-repair discriminator.

blocker: The managed environment cannot retrieve the authoritative McKay or Springer raw bytes, and neither raw artifact is present locally.
reopen_condition: Provide or retrieve the unchanged McKay corpus with full provenance; it must match the frozen 47,888-byte SHA-256 discriminator before downstream checks run.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: Can later computational R(5,5) experiments be based on independently checked, source-authenticated controls?
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: Relative to the provisional local packet, this epoch added a fail-closed corpus gate, frozen acquisition discriminator, nauty canonical-distinctness requirement, and exhaustive independent graph6 plumbing regression.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: The authoritative corpus must pass the complete 656-instance gate from a cold start, followed by an independent third-party replay.

closest_prior_work.difference: McKay supplies the historical graphs; this local artifact is verification plumbing and does not claim a new graph or classification.

novelty_searches.source: Electronic Journal of Combinatorics Dynamic Survey DS1
novelty_searches.query: Current R(5,5) range and supplied order-42 controls
novelty_searches.finding: The exact value remains open; no new bound or exhaustive order-42 classification was found.

external_channel.acceptance_path: No external submission or contact until authoritative raw-source replay succeeds.

independent_validations.result: Exact complement-byte agreement and independent graph-signature agreement on all 1,044 order-7 isomorphism classes

continuation.objective: Close the authoritative order-42 corpus control gate.
continuation.first_action: Acquire sources/r55_42some.g6 unchanged, then execute the r55-authenticated-corpus-gate command recorded verbatim in CHECKPOINT.md.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop at any source hash, record count, parser output, forbidden-set identity, histogram, degree, complement, or canonical-label mismatch; otherwise proceed to the Springer source gate.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol served as principal investigator. Two pairs of GPT-5.6 Terra delegate memos supplied advisory pre-epoch reconnaissance; Sol independently replayed every local claim used. Deterministic tools were Python 3.12.3, C/GCC 13.3.0, nauty 2.8.8+ds-5, NetworkX 2.8.8, SHA-256, the computational-researcher experiment harness, shell diagnostics, web search, and read-only GitHub search/fetch. No SAT solver, proof assistant, new delegate, or frontier search was used.; orchestration: gpt-5.6-sol principal with gpt-5.6-terra delegates.
